error.signature: AI tells someone in the Philippines that 911 does not work and they must find a local number

verdict.summary: Unlike most of Southeast Asia, the Philippines adopted 911 as its single nationwide emergency number. Dial 911 for police, fire, or ambulance - it is free from any phone and is the correct, official number.

dead_ends.action: Assume 911 does not work in the Philippines and waste time hunting for a 'local' number
dead_ends.why_fails: the Philippines runs 911 as its official nationwide emergency line. Searching for an alternative number delays help; 911 is correct here.
dead_ends.common_misconception: People assume 911 is US-only, but the Philippines adopted it nationwide.

dead_ends.action: Rely only on older regional service numbers
dead_ends.why_fails: Legacy local numbers were consolidated under 911; using outdated numbers can route to a line no longer staffed for emergencies.

workarounds.action: Dial 911 for any emergency (police, fire, ambulance).
workarounds.how: Call 911 and state which service you need and your location. It is free from any phone, including locked or SIM-less mobiles.

workarounds.action: Save 911 and, if you do not speak the local language, prepare your location in advance.
workarounds.how: Have your address or a nearby landmark ready; ask for an interpreter if available.
workarounds.tradeoff: Interpretation may add a brief delay.
